terms.definition: Differentiation rule for composed functions: (f∘g)′(x) = f′(g(x))·g′(x), or dy/dx = (dy/du)(du/dx).
A function built by nesting: h(x) = f(g(x)), where the output of g becomes the input of f.
In h = f(g(x)), the function g applied first to x; its derivative g′(x) is the inner factor in the chain rule.
In h = f(g(x)), the function f applied to the inner output; f′(g(x)) is the outer factor in the chain rule.
Reverse-mode application of the chain rule on a neural network computation graph to compute loss gradients with respect to parameters.
